title: Batch Normalization: Accelerating Deep Network Training by Reducing Internal Covariate Shift
summary: Introduced batch normalization, a technique that normalizes layer inputs by re-centering and re-scaling during training. This addressed the 'internal covariate shift' problem and allowed much higher learning rates, faster convergence, and reduced sensitivity to weight initialization. Batch normalization became a standard component in virtually all deep neural networks.
key_contributions: Introduced batch normalization as a trainable layer that normalizes activations
Enabled higher learning rates and faster training convergence
Reduced the need for careful weight initialization
Acted as a regularizer, sometimes eliminating the need for dropout
impact: Batch normalization became one of the most universally adopted techniques in deep learning, used in nearly every major architecture from ResNet to modern GANs. Later variants (layer normalization, group normalization) adapted the concept for transformers and other architectures.
query_hooks: How does batch norm help training?
